# Zeta Trigonometry

To describe zeta, you need to use zeta itself. <br />
To clearly understand what I mean, you need to replace all formulas that use exp with zeta.

```math
\text{zsin}(z) = \frac{\zeta(iz) - \zeta(-iz)}{2i}
```

```math
\text{zcos}(z) = \frac{\zeta(iz) + \zeta(-iz)}{2}
```

```math
\text{zsinh}(z) = \frac{\zeta(z) - \zeta(-z)}{2}
```

```math
\text{zcosh}(z) = \frac{\zeta(z) + \zeta(-z)}{2}
```

<br />

```math
zsin(−z)=−zsin(z)
```

```math
zcos(−z)=zcos(z)
```

```math
zsinh(−z)=−zsinh(z)
```

```math
zcosh(−z)=zcosh(z)
```

<br />

```math
\zeta(-iz) = zcos(z) - i * zsin(z)
```

```math
\zeta(iz) = zcos(z) + i * zsin(z)
```

```math
\zeta(-z) = zcosh(z) - zsinh(z)
```

```math
\zeta(z) = zcosh(z) + zsinh(z)
```

<br />

```math
zeta(0) = zcos(0) = zcosh(0) = -1/2
```

<br />

```math
\zeta(iz)\zeta(-iz) = \text{zcos}^2(z) + \text{zsin}^2(z)
```

```math
\zeta(z)\zeta(-z) = \text{zcosh}^2(z) - \text{zsinh}^2(z)
```

Zsin all zeros can be obtained through:

```math
Im(\zeta(i z))
```

Zcos all zeros can be obtained through:

```math
Re(\zeta(i z))
```

<br />
Zeta all zeros can be obtained through:

```math
Im(\zeta(i z) * \zeta(-i z))
```

<br />

```python
def find_zeros():
    step = prev = cur = 0.0
    while True:
        step += 0.1
        t = step + 0.5j
        if prev * cur < 0.0:
            zerot = findroot(lambda x: im(zeta(1j * x) * zeta(-x * 1j)), (t - 0.1, t))
            print(zerot)
        prev = cur
        cur = im(zeta(1j * t) * zeta(-t * 1j))

find_zeros()
```

<br />

If we take the derivative of zsin using exp, then the function becomes non-closed. The question is, what will the exp derivative tend to if we take it an infinite number of times from zsin?

```math
L(z) =  \frac{\zeta'(z)}{\zeta(z)}
```

Hypothesis:
The zeros of the zeta cosine are successively greater than the zeros of the zeta sine. The question is, is this ever violated other than the first time?

zsin(9.7859575487180984754087707349572202650536044454741) <br />
zcos(13.313727465338546840370551848965717592371385169142) <br />
zsin(14.218916287245118538854216319543702469790394964131) <br />
zcos(15.396602022366452188684401838753663894954581856449) <br />
zsin(17.858842108570570753141317568581125429075310852149) <br />
zcos(19.969277330847164008092660957449002016900807761801) <br />
zsin(20.930100613681927365612187289676853134924003353665) <br />

```js
<script src="./zcos-zeros.js"></script>
<script src="./zsin-zeros.js"></script>
<script>
    for (let i = 1; i < 20001; i++) {
        console.log(zsin[i] <= zcos[i]); // 20k all true except first
    }
</script>
```
